CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2018 Continued To Dec-19-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT RARECULTURE LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend as to each cause of action. The court sustains the demurrer as to the First Cause of Action with leave to amend because Plaintiffs fail to allege facts specifically alleging that Defendant provided the allegedly unsafe scaffolding or exercised control over it. (McKown v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. (2002) 27 Cal.4th 219, 225.) The court sustains the demurrer as to the Second Cause of Action with leave to amend because Plaintiffs fail to allege facts that Defendant retained control over the worksite in a way that affirmatively contributed to the injury. (Kinsman v. Unocal Corp. (2005) 37 Cal.App.4th 1439, 1446.) Passive failure to exercise retained control does not constitute an affirmative contribution to the injuries; and Plaintiffs do not allege facts that Defendant agreed to provide safety measures. (Tverberg v. Fillner Constr. Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1439, 1446.) The court sustains the demurrer as to the Third Cause of Action with leave to amend. Plaintiffs' allegations are supported by information in their opposition that is not present in the complaint. Plaintiffs allege that they are unlicensed painters and employees of Defendant and allege that Defendant is uninsured in violation of Labor Code section 3706. Under Blew v. Horner (1986) 187 Cal. App.3d 1380, 1382-83 and Labor Code Section 2750.5, unlicensed contractors who would normally require a license are employees as a matter of law. However, the allegation regarding Labor Code section 2750.5 and Plaintiffs being employees as a matter of law does not appear in the complaint. Plaintiffs should amend their complaint to more specifically plead allegations of the employer-employee relationship and how that triggers the alleged statutory violation for the lack of workers compensation insurance.
The court sustains the demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action with leave to amend. Plaintiffs fail to allege any facts to support the claim that Defendant designed or manufactured the scaffolding or introduced it into the stream of commerce. (Cadlo v. Owens-Ill. Inc., (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 513, 516 [holding that a strict products liability claim requires an allegation that defendant had an actual connection with the design, manufacture, or distribution of the defective product.])
The court sustains the demurrer as to the Fifth Cause of Action with leave to amend. Plaintiffs allege that scaffolding was provided by NorCal Rental Group and supplied by Centric Construction, Inc., not by RAREculture. A defendant cannot be liable for defects in another manufacturer's product. (O'Neil v. Crane Co. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 335, 351.) As currently pleaded, Defendant cannot be found liable for negligent failure to warn.
Plaintiff must file her amended complaint no later than 20 days from the date of this order. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/ac
